summary: Chemistry-solver re-test, multi-focal Phase C architecture (May 2026)
verdict: PERSISTS at Track CD level (+0.054 bohr/n_max); first-row no-op of Phase C closures

baseline_pk_composed_v2_0_32.note: Level-4 PK-composed solver (composed_diatomic.py), DIFFERENT solver from balanced coupled

n_max=3_screened_on.status: in flight at memo time; expected: bit-identical to Track CD baseline (W1c is no-op for first-row LiH)

w1c_bit_identical_lih.interpretation: Phase C-W1c diagnostic claim verified: cross_center_screened_vne._detect_core_type returns None for Z<11, screened path collapses to bare path bit-precisely

architectural_finding: Multi-focal Phase C closures (W1a, W1b, W1c, W2b-easy) do NOT change the chemistry-solver output for first-row LiH/BeH2/H2O.
W1a, W1b live on the qubit nuclear-electronic two-register architecture (Track NI, Sprint MH).
W1c is structurally relevant only for second-row (Z>=11) systems with [Ne]+ frozen cores.
W2b-easy (Paper 39 tensor-product propinquity) is a theoretical-mathematical theorem, not a code change.
The chemistry-solver upgrade the framework received is the balanced-coupled construction itself (Track CD, v2.0.39, April 2026), which shipped BEFORE Phase C.
Track CD reduced the PK-composed wall (+0.18 bohr/l_max) to +0.054 bohr/n_max (3x improvement).
implications: The remaining R_eq drift is intrinsic to balanced coupled in the qubit basis at small n_max.
Single-center hydrogenic basis flexibility per block cannot resolve nuclear geometry to better than 7-9% at equilibrium.
Phase C closures are a no-op for first-row chemistry; the architectural upgrade for chemistry shipped in v2.0.39.
Recommended next test: NaH PES with screened=True + coupled_fci_energy (cheap, <5 min compute). Phase C D-PES regression confirmed 5.4-6.0x cross-V_ne reduction but couldn't compute binding.
Recommended speculative direction: multi-focal V_ee operator (e-e analog of W1a's e-N closure).
